title: nepeta kurdica
intro: Nepeta kurdica, also known as Kurdish catmint, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, native to the mountainous regions of Turkey and Iraq. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Nepeta kurdica include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and stomach cramps, as well as its use in reducing anxiety and stress. Nepeta kurdica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe digestive issues and promote relaxation.
